---
title: "Moving out for a better career: Parental co-residence and later earnings"
output: html_notebook
---

## Naive model

$$
\log(\text{income}_{i,t+5}) = \beta_0 + \beta_1 \,\text{coresidence}_{it} + \beta_2 X_{it} + \epsilon_{it}
$$

where:

-   $i$ is the individual;

-   $t$ is the year;

-   $\text{income}$ is the income earned by individual $i$ in year $t$ in Euro;

-   $\text{coresidence}$ is the number of years the individual lived with their parents from age 18 to age 23;

-   $X$ is a vector of control variables.

$\hat{\beta}_1$ is likely biased, because parental co-residence during college years is not random. More conscientious individuals, for example, may prefer to live with their parents and will also earn more later, on average.

## IV-DiD

First stage:

$$
\text{coresidence}_{it} = \pi \left( \text{treat}_i \times \text{post}_t \right) + \gamma_i + \delta_t + u_{it}
$$

where:

-   $i$ is the individual;

-   $t$ is the year;

-   $\text{treat}$ is the treatment dummy;

-   $\text{post}$ is the

Second stage:L

$$
Y_{it} = \beta \widehat{CoRes}_{it} + \gamma_i + \delta_t + \epsilon_{it}
$$
